title: Sinusitis (starter evidence summary)
text:
Most acute sinus symptoms are viral and improve with time; antibiotics are not always needed.
Telephone triage focuses on severity, duration, and complications.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Swelling around the eye, vision changes, or pain with eye movement (possible orbital complication)
- Confusion, stiff neck, severe headache, or new neurologic deficits

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- High fever or severe facial pain
- Immunocompromised patients with significant symptoms
- Symptoms that are severe or worsening rather than improving

Supportive care (if mild/moderate):
- Hydration, rest, saline irrigation, and symptom control if safe.
- Seek in-person care if symptoms persist, worsen, or red flags develop.
